findings.problem: The closing movement disperses onto queue management and follow-on claimants after the chapter's central fracture is already visible. Jamila's move toward Mofiz's camp and Rahima's willingness to stand witness are the emotionally consequential turns, but the ending lands on continuing throughput instead of the sharpest irreversible relationship change, so the chapter reads as setup rather than a fully cut scene-end.
findings.rewrite_direction: Refocus the ending around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:263, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:307, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:347 so the final beat lands on one central irreversible choice: Jamila visibly joining the clean-papers camp, Rahima becoming the first named witness on Nura's page, or Halima registering the personal cost of that split. Keep sign-up logistics only where they force that choice on page.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the final 3 to 5 paragraphs beginning around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:325 end on a changed relationship or allegiance involving Halima, Jamila, Rahima, Kashem, or Nura; fail if the last image is still primarily a continuation-of-processing beat such as another generic claimant arriving.

findings.problem: Once Aminul admits that competing witness stories can endanger supposedly clean claims, the chapter has already exposed the real knife. The back third keeps restating the same split through Halima's naming speech, crowd echoes, list headings, and children's chants instead of making each new beat cash out into a fresh cost. Escalation turns into reinforcement.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the post-revelation movement at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:181, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:203, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:221, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:291, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:343. Cut at least one reiteration of the faction labels or one explanatory restatement, and spend the recovered space on a new concrete consequence such as a spouse breaking ranks, a claimant withdrawing, or a witness realizing exactly what testimony will cost.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if every major beat after snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:181 introduces a new behavior, loss, or allegiance change on the page; fail if two consecutive beats after that line merely rename the same house-list versus blood-list split without adding new material consequence.

findings.problem: The narration slips out of Halima-anchored close third into direct declarations about other characters' interior states: Majed's fear logic, Aminul feeling procedure slipping, Jamila treating composure as her blade, and Nura being angry, frightened, and overprepared. The style bible allows selective opening to Nura or Kashem, not free access to multiple minds inside the same Halima scene, so these lines blur narrative discipline.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the mind-reading at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:185, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:285 through Halima's observed deductions or through physical evidence only. If a line must stay interior, keep it attached to Halima's own consciousness rather than asserting Majed, Aminul, Jamila, or Nura's private state as omniscient fact.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised spans can all be read as Halima's perception or inference and there are no declarative sentences in those locations that state another character's private fear, motive, or coping method as omniscient fact; fail if Majed, Aminul, Jamila, or Nura are still directly mind-read in Halima's scene.

findings.problem: A repeated 'X did not ...' refusal construction becomes a noticeable prose tic in the chapter's pressure beats. Because so many key turns are phrased through what a character does not do or does not look at, withholding starts to sound mechanically similar instead of socially specific.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the refusal beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:263, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:321 so at least several are rendered through affirmative gesture, posture, eye-line, or material interaction rather than the repeated 'did not' template. Differentiate Mofiz's avoidance, Jamila's control, Halima's steadiness, and Nura's braced silence with distinct observable choices.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least 3 of the 5 cited beats are no longer built on an explicit 'did not' refusal and each revised beat conveys a different social pressure by observable action; fail if the same negative-syntax template still carries most of those moments.

summary: No continuity contradiction, tense drift, sanitization drift, or dialogue-register failure surfaced in this pass; the voices are distinct and contraction use is broadly consistent with the style bible. The chapter still fails overall because the back third overstates a split the reader already understands, the ending lands on process instead of the sharpest relational consequence, and the prose slips outside Halima-anchored close third while leaning repeatedly on the same negative-refusal construction.
